items.title: We're Initiating Coverage of SpaceX at Buy
items.summary: We're initiating coverage of SpaceX with a Buy rating and $200 target price, implying ~25% upside by year's end. We see our target price as a milestone on the way to $900+, a realistic goal assuming key engineering achievements are demonstrated at scale. We offer further analysis herein.

items.title: U.S. Diversified Banks: Weighing the Cross-Currents
items.summary: Banks face a critical inflection point on balance-sheet repricing dynamics; in this Deep Dive, we investigate which banks look best positioned for a higher-for-longer backdrop with a steeper yield curve, while managing offsetting deposit pricing pressures.

items.title: Taiwan Electronic Components & Equipment: Riding the Growth Cycle
items.summary: We believe AI infrastructure is entering a new power architecture cycle, with a structural shift to rack-level backup power creating a multiyear growth opportunity that remains underappreciated by the market. We initiate coverage of Dynapack at Buy with a target price of NT$800.

items.title: Taiwan Semiconductors: Riding on Google
items.summary: Investors have been optimistic about AI ASIC surpassing GPU in AI computing. But a host of factors has us expecting Taiwanese design-service companies with better supply-chain management capability to stand out with positive growth outlooks into 2027; Mediatek tops our preferred names.

items.title: World Radar Screen: July Update
items.summary: Value–Momentum performance was mixed across regions in June. MSCI Europe ex-UK, Japan and Emerging Markets led regional gains, while Asia ex Japan and the UK saw sharp declines. The broader aggregate AC World strategy remained positive, returning 1.8% for the month and 13% year to date.

items.title: U.S. Rates Strategy: A Lack of Near-Term Catalysts
items.summary: We continue to forecast lower yields by year's end, but note a lack of near-term bullish catalysts. Treasury yields are moving higher, due to inflation and continued strong jobs data; we disagree with the market pricing in a decline in the unemployment rate and two rate hikes.

items.title: U.S. Semis and Equipment: 2Q26 Earnings Preview
items.summary: We expect further earnings upside across semis and semi equipment names, supported by improving end demand and reasonable valuations despite strong SOX performance. We favor AMD, TXN and AMAT into earnings. We also add an upside Catalyst Watch on MU and a downside Catalyst Watch on QCOM.

items.title: U.S. Light Vehicle Sales & Inventory Update
items.summary: June U.S. light vehicle sales came in at the high end of expectations, with demand remaining strong and pricing supported by disciplined inventory levels. We view recent demand and pricing trends as positive for auto dealers heading into earnings season. We break down the trends herein.

items.title: Pan-Asia Focus List: Third-Quarter Refresh
items.summary: We refresh our Pan-Asia Focus List for the third quarter with our analysts’ most non-consensus, high-conviction Buy and Sell ideas. New Buy ideas are Geely, ICICI Bank, ASMPT, Samsung Electronics, Hengli Hydraulic, Keppel, Ajinomoto, Fanuc, CATL, Kingboard Laminates and Kia.

items.title: Continental Nears Deal to Sell ContiTech for €4bn
items.summary: Continental is nearing a deal to sell ContiTech for about €4bn, broadly in line with our expectations. We believe the sale could lift the Buy-rated shares toward our €80 target price, supported by potential value realization, a strong dividend yield and attractive shareholder returns.

items.title: European Gaming: Taking Stock After the World Cup Group Stages
items.summary: Handle growth appears strong for sports betting operators following the World Cup group stages. However, results have been mixed for margins, with favorites winning more often than in 2022, though a high number of draws may provide some offset. We explore further implications herein.

items.title: TSMC: 2Q26 Analyst Meeting Preview
items.summary: We think TSMC may further raise its 2026 growth target and long-term revenue outlook, supported by strong AI-driven demand. Margins should remain resilient, utilization broad-based, and wafer pricing on an uptrend. We continue to view TSMC as a key beneficiary of AI semiconductor growth.

items.title: Global Macro Strategy: Growing pAIns
items.summary: After June NFP, a near-term Fed hike looks much less likely. We take profits on USDNOK, Kospi and EM Asia positions. We stay overweight U.S. equities and tactically long Europe. Copper’s breakout vs gold may not be a bullish cyclical signal. EMFX carry faces crowdedness and JPY risks.

items.title: European Economics: The Return of Political Risk in Italy
items.summary: Italy’s recent political stability has helped bring foreign investors back. Is the next general election in 2027 going to throw this stability off course? We examine this and other key questions around the 2027 general election, including timing, likely outcomes and policy implications.

items.title: European Rates Weekly: Supply and Demand Themes for H2
items.summary: This week, We focus on H2 supply and demand dynamics. EGB and SSA issuance should remain strong, though SSA net funding needs will ease. On the demand side, changes to reserve requirements and UK bank capital rules could materially affect demand for EGBs and gilts. More details herein.

items.title: Metal Matters: An Existential Moment for the Aluminum Bull Market
items.summary: We update our aluminum forecasts, as we now see prices bottoming in the coming month before recovering toward $3,300 to $3,500 a ton late this year. We don't see aluminum as an attractive bear trade from current levels, and in fact recommend buying the dip over the summer into year's end.

items.title: Oil Monitor: Crude Oil Remains Under Pressure
items.summary: Fundamentals are rapidly reasserting themselves as Hormuz disruptions fade, with Brent back to the low $70s a barrel. We expect further de-escalation in the coming months and continue to recommend selling any summer rallies. We forecast Brent reaching $60 to $65 a barrel by year's end.

items.title: U.S. Economics Weekly: The Case for Hikes Was Just Revised Away
items.summary: The latest jobs data will relieve hawkish concerns about rate hikes. With headline inflation having peaked and core PCE set to be revised lower in September, any case for hikes is disappearing; in our base case, rising unemployment in coming months spurs a return to Fed cuts in October.

items.title: PULSE Monitor: Volatility Gets a Yellow Card
items.summary: VIX isn't far from 52-week lows, but VIXEQ, a gauge of average single-stock implied volatility, has steadily risen to near 52-week highs, a setup that's led to noticeable style and factor rotations. We see rotational chop lingering, but this creates an opportunity for less crowded trades.

items.title: Emerging Markets Strategy Weekly: Scores on the Doors
items.summary: On a year-to-date cumulative total return basis, emerging-markets local currency bonds have continued to outperform USTs, underscoring the relative attractiveness of carry and selective FX tailwinds across the region. Latin America remains the clear winner; we offer more details herein.

items.title: North America: Road Ahead Second-Half 2026
items.summary: We remain constructive on the full-year U.S. equity outlook, and expect the broadening theme to assert itself in the second half. While AI tailwinds will remain critical to the U.S. equity narrative, we see opportunity in stock selection across industries, with more detail in this note.

items.title: A Stable Labor Market, But Not a Strong One
items.summary: Payroll job growth slowed to 57,000 in June, while the unemployment rate declined, but alongside a large decline in labor-force participation. We continue to think the low-hiring environment will imply further weakening in job growth and rising unemployment later in the year.

items.title: Quantitative Global Macro Strategy: Previewing Payrolls Days
items.summary: Treasuries typically sell off on the median payrolls day, with bearish surprises in rates showing mean reversion approximately 15 days later. Equities tend to rally on payrolls days, but their performance is highly conditional on the reaction in USTs. We look at more patterns in this note.

items.title: U.S. Aerospace & Defense: "Aerospace First, Defense Next"
items.summary: Our second-quarter tactical framework has been "Aerospace First, Defense Next" given our belief that aerospace stocks would lead the group out of its post-conflict pullback. We now see risk/reward as increasingly asymmetric across defense stocks as well. We offer further details herein.

items.title: Global Gas and Power Insights: American Exceptionalism!
items.summary: Strong U.S. LNG exports should continue to loosen up LNG and European gas supply-demand balances over the next 12 to 18 months. Meanwhile, the U.S. gas balance would be marginally tighter, though still overwhelmed by strong production expected from the Permian and Haynesville.

items.title: The Speculative Grade Beat: Why We're Cutting Duration
items.summary: Base rates — not spreads — account for the lion's share of high-yield total yield, a dynamic whose effects we explore in this note. Having flipped our Asset Allocation preference to loans, we now shorten duration in our High Yield Factor Framework by moving to underweight Long Duration.

items.title: U.S. High Grade: Fundamentals Wrap-Up
items.summary: Our first-quarter review of investment-grade issuers’ credit fundamental trends shows a capex cycle that keeps accelerating, with the trailing 12-month capex share of EBITDA reaching 49.2%, while the broader market’s balance sheet stays remarkably intact. We offer further review herein.

items.title: Japan FX: Nobody's Stopping the Yen's Depreciation
items.summary: The rise in Japanese interest rates is tapering off, which should support gains by the JPY over the longer term. But it wouldn't be surprising if it also dampened the motivation of the Takaichi government and U.S. authorities to minimize the decline in the JPY, as we explain in this note.

items.title: Latin America Economic Outlook & Strategy: A Shift Right and Reform
items.summary: Latin America's political shift to the right improves the outlook for reform but doesn't guarantee it, as anti-incumbent sentiment has created fragmented governments that lack the legislative support to pass crucial and often unpopular fiscal reforms. We offer further analysis herein.
